statement.p: You will build a rectangular sheep enclosure next to a river. There is no need to build a fence along the river, so you only need to build on three sides. You have a total of 410 feet of fence to use. Find the dimensions of the pen such that you can enclose the maximum possible area. One approach is to let x represent the length of fencing that runs perpendicular to the river, and write an expression for the area of the enclosure. Then find its vertex and interpret it.
The length of the pen (parallel to the river) should be , the width (perpendicular to the river) should be , and the maximum possible area is .

solution.p: Drawing a diagram should help us write an expression for the area of the pen.
Let the width be w ft, and the length be \ell ft. Since there are only three sides of the fence, we have:
If the pens width is w feet, then its length is 410-2w feet. Now we can write an expression for the area of the pen:
Identify that a=-2, b=410, and c=0. The maximum area happens at the parabolas vertex.
To find the vertex, we first find the parabolas axis of symmetry by using the axis formula:
Next, we substitute w= 102.5 into the expression, and we have:

The parabolas vertex is at {\left(102.5,21012.5\right)}. This implies that the pens maximum area is {21012.5} square feet. This happens when the width is 102.5 feet, and the length is 410 - 2(102.5), or 205, feet.
So when the length is 205 feet and the width is 102.5 feet, the pen has a maximum possible area of {21012.5} square feet.
The following graph illustrates our findings.
